title: Leg cramps during pregnancy
excerpt: Why calf cramps happen at night and what may help with stretching, hydration, and magnesium-rich foods — when to mention them at prenatal visits.
content:
## Symptoms

Sudden tight pain in the calf or foot, often at night in the second or third trimester.

## What may help

- Gentle calf stretches before bed.
- Stay hydrated; balanced electrolytes from food.
- Foods with magnesium: pumpkin seeds, spinach, bananas, whole grains (as part of normal diet).
- Comfortable shoes; avoid standing too long.

## Notes

Persistent swelling with headache or vision changes is **not** a simple cramp — seek urgent care for possible preeclampsia screening.
metaTitle: Leg cramps during pregnancy | Bầu Ăn Gì? Blog
metaDescription: Causes and relief for pregnancy leg cramps — stretching, fluids, and foods; not a substitute for clinical advice.
